baseline: OpenToonz 1.7.1 / Qt 5; codex/qt-6-port reviewed at c4d5daf48; regular Linux, macOS, and Windows branch CI green on 2026-06-27; latest Qt 6 Experimental Binary Builds result still failed on 2026-06-22 with Windows CMake and Linux compile blockers while macOS arm64 packaged; Project Sync skips until QT6_PROJECT_TOKEN is configured

goals.title: Restore macOS Qt 6 CI guard preflight
goals.body: Superseded by later regular branch CI. Linux, macOS, and Windows builds were green at codex/qt-6-port commit b49ed0df5 in the latest observed runs, so the prior macOS rg preflight blocker is accepted unless it regresses.

goals.title: Validate latest local Qt 6 branch commit
goals.body: codex/qt-6-port was reviewed at c4d5daf48, and the latest observed regular branch CI for Linux, macOS, and Windows succeeded for that commit on 2026-06-27.

goals.title: Verify Qt 6 Experimental Binary Builds after aqt module fix
goals.body: The latest observed Qt 6 Experimental Binary Builds workflow result failed on 2026-06-22. The earlier Linux aqt module-list blocker is no longer the current failure: Windows x64 still fails during CMake configuration because the requested Visual Studio 17 2022 generator is not found on the scheduled runner, and Linux x86_64 AppImage fails compiling iwa_bokehreffx.cpp because UCHAR_MAX is used without the header that defines it. macOS arm64 built, verified, created a DMG, and uploaded an artifact, but the workflow still failed overall and skipped prerelease publication.

goals.title: Fix Linux Qt 6 experimental bokeh reference compile
goals.body: Linux x86_64 AppImage packaging failed in the latest experimental workflow while compiling iwa_bokehreffx.cpp because UCHAR_MAX is used without including the header that defines it. The current Qt 6 branch still lacks that include. Add the missing include and rerun the experimental package workflow.

goals.title: Land Qt 6 experimental workflow runner fix on master
goals.body: The scheduled experimental workflow runs from the default-branch workflow file. The latest run failed Windows CMake configuration when Visual Studio 17 2022 could not be found on the scheduled runner. The current Qt 6 branch workflow uses the Windows 2022 runner and expanded Qt module list, while master still differs. Land the runner or generator fix on master before expecting scheduled Windows packages to pass.

goals.title: Maintain Qt deprecated API build lanes
goals.body: Keep the Qt 5 deprecated-API build guard and the Qt 6 deprecated-API build lane reproducible. Branch documentation records that the Qt 6 lane compiled and linked OpenToonz without Qt deprecation errors or Qt-related deprecated-API warnings; rerun after shared source changes.

goals.title: Enable Qt 6 Project Sync token
goals.body: The Qt 6 Project Sync workflow is present and scheduled, and it reads manual-goals JSON, but current runs still skip Project updates because QT6_PROJECT_TOKEN is not configured. Add the repository secret before treating GitHub Project status as live.

goals.title: Smoke-test macOS arm64 Qt 6 experimental DMG
goals.body: The latest observed experimental workflow produced a macOS arm64 DMG artifact even though Windows and Linux failed. Smoke-test package mechanics, app launch, runtime data, Qt plugins, multimedia backends, and basic scene lifecycle from that artifact, but do not treat it as cross-platform release readiness.

goals.title: Startup/workspace smoke: isolated profile launch
goals.body: Launch the packaged Qt 6 app with an isolated runtime profile, confirm the intended instance is under test, create and save a scene, relaunch, reopen it, switch rooms, and capture startup/workspace screenshots once a current package or local bundle is available. Report docs: doc/qt6_port_progress/2026-06-29-functional-gaps.md and doc/qt6_port_progress/2026-06-29-manual-verification-checklist.md.

goals.title: Verify package runtime data and plugin loading
goals.body: Confirm packaged apps load Qt frameworks, Qt plugins, SVG/icons, multimedia backends, stuff data, rooms, QSS, shaders, brushes, presets, and default projects from the package rather than a developer environment.

goals.title: Viewer/rendering parity: default scene and viewer crop
goals.body: Compare the Qt 6 viewer against the Qt 5 baseline for default scene display, blank/nonblank viewer behavior, stale framebuffer behavior, high-DPI alignment, and screenshot or pixel crop evidence on representative raster, vector, Toonz Raster, composite, and FX-heavy scenes.

goals.title: Verify manual Preview and FX Preview UI workflows
goals.body: Exercise File > Preview, schematic FX Preview command, live playback and timer behavior, sub-camera dragging, previewed-frame save UI, overwrite and warning dialogs, cancellation, invalid output paths, and production FX-heavy preview scenes.

goals.title: Verify final render parity on production scenes
goals.body: Render representative raster, vector, Toonz Raster, composite, frame-sequence, and FX-heavy scenes. Compare dimensions, frame count, alpha/background handling, color, output file naming, external-viewer behavior, and Qt 5 output.

goals.title: Drawing/input parity: raster/vector strokes and tablet basics
goals.body: Validate repeated raster and vector drawing input, cursor/stroke alignment, undo/redo, save/reopen, redraw behavior, pressure-sensitive tablet behavior, and obvious latency regressions. Synthetic tablet-event smokes are not enough for hardware parity.

goals.title: Verify real OS-level mouse and cursor delivery
goals.body: Rerun the real desktop input path in an unlocked, frontmost user session and verify drawing, transform drags, context menus, cursor feedback, and event delivery. Prior app-side and synthetic smokes do not prove real operating-system input delivery.

goals.title: Verify Selection and Animate/Edit tool parity
goals.body: Rerun Codex smokes where possible, then have a human verify rectangular, freehand, and polyline selection, transform handles, mode variants, modifiers, active-axis dragging, undo/redo, and cursor feedback against Qt 5 behavior.

goals.title: Timeline/xsheet smoke: navigation, selection, playback basics
goals.body: Smoke frame navigation, cell selection, playback controls, note editing, column movement, panning, auto-pan, context menus, and common xsheet/timeline interactions for Qt 6 regressions.

goals.title: Verify onion skin guides rulers and overlays
goals.body: Manually verify onion-skin marker workflows, fixed and relative markers, Shift and Trace, colors, orientations, camera box, safe area, field guides, ruler and guide variants, and overlay coexistence with drawing, room changes, DPI settings, and stylesheets.

goals.title: Palette/styles smoke: style selection and color edit behavior
goals.body: Validate style selection, color editing, palette panel interactions, page and tab behavior, context menus, style chooser behavior, and any Qt 6 visual or signal/slot regressions.

goals.title: Expand and maintain QJSEngine script compatibility evidence
goals.body: Keep the current bounded and natural-exit script-smoke fixtures green while maintaining the script-smoke registry guard, GUI-smoke registry guard, and strict Qt 6 script guards. Recent coverage includes global-output formatting, run(FilePath), FilePath mutation and metadata, Scene column and level-name cases, Level name coercion, Image load replacement, and Transform chaining/validation. Add coverage for remaining advanced scene APIs, additional object bindings, and external scripts users depend on.

goals.title: Verify real user scripts against Qt 6 QJSEngine
goals.body: Run representative studio or user scripts against the Qt 6 QJSEngine runtime, not only synthetic smoke fixtures. Include path-heavy, scene-mutation, renderer, wrapper-lifetime, and expected-error scripts where possible, and compare behavior with Qt 5 where practical.

goals.title: Verify Script Console GUI workflows
goals.body: Open the Script Console and verify broader interactive command editing, run() GUI-console error paths, view(Image), view(Level), invalid argument cases, FlipBook cleanup, visible error reporting, repeated console sessions, stale-state cleanup, and external script behavior against Qt 5 where practical.

goals.title: Verify audio recording playback and lip sync UI
goals.body: Run product-level audio workflows on real hardware, including recording, save, insert, audible playback, permission prompts, cancellation, selected output route, and lip-sync preview/timing. Backend and WAV-writer smokes are preflight only, and structured microphone timeout skips are not recording parity.

goals.title: Verify camera preview and stop motion capture
goals.body: Run live camera and stop-motion workflows on real hardware, including device enumeration, preview, still capture, scene insertion, device changes, and hotplug behavior.

goals.title: Verify tablet stylus pressure tilt and Windows pointer bridge
goals.body: Test pressure, tilt, hover, eraser, side buttons, device switching, latency, and Windows pointer-input bridge behavior on real tablet or stylus hardware.

goals.title: Validate Linux Windows and macOS Qt 6 release packages
goals.body: Validate Linux AppImage, Windows package, macOS DMG, deploy-tool arguments, Qt module lists, plugins, multimedia backends, runtime library paths, helper executables, OpenGL behavior, and file associations after the Qt 6 Experimental Binary Builds workflow produces fresh cross-platform artifacts. The latest observed run packaged macOS arm64 but failed Windows and Linux, so release-package validation remains blocked.

goals.title: Verify runtime translation and language switching
goals.body: Build translations, package them, switch UI language, restart, and spot-check localized UI. The Qt 6 translation build lane has prior evidence, but packaged runtime language switching remains a manual release check.

goals.title: Define Qt 6 release candidate signoff criteria
goals.body: Create and review release-candidate validation criteria covering supported platforms, Qt 5 baseline version, representative production scenes, known limitations, release blockers, user-facing docs, developer docs, and troubleshooting updates.

goals.title: Studio field-test pass: creative workflow acceptance
goals.body: Reserve a human field-test pass for real creative workflows once CI, Codex smoke evidence, and targeted human smoke items are no longer blocking. Use representative production scenes and record accepted limitations explicitly.
